title: glechoma teucrioides
intro: Glechoma teucrioides, also known as Creeping Lemon, is a perennial herb native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and distinctive lemon-scented leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Glechoma teucrioides include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits, including reduced stress, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function.
